overview:
The AZ-305 exam validates the skills required to design infrastructure solutions, data storage, business continuity, and identity and governance. It focuses on the ability to design a comprehensive solution that meets business requirements and aligns with the Microsoft Azure Well-Architected Framework.

This exam is intended for experienced IT professionals who have a deep understanding of Azure services and can translate business requirements into a technical architecture. It is the final step to earning the Microsoft Certified: Azure Solutions Architect Expert certification.
recertification_info: Renew the certification for free via Microsoft Learn by passing an online renewal assessment every year after the initial certification.

official_resources.title: Azure Solutions Architect Expert Learning Path

preparation_tips: Master the Azure Well-Architected Framework, specifically the five pillars: Reliability, Security, Cost Optimization, Operational Excellence, and Performance Efficiency.
Deep dive into Azure Virtual Network (VNet) peering, Hub-and-Spoke topologies, and Azure Firewall configurations.
Compare and contrast different storage options: Azure Blob Storage, Azure Files, Azure NetApp Files, and Azure Data Lake Storage.
Understand the nuances between Azure SQL Database, Azure SQL Managed Instance, and SQL Server on VMs.
Study identity management strategies using Azure Active Directory (Entra ID), including Conditional Access and PIM.
Practice designing high availability (HA) and disaster recovery (DR) strategies using Azure Site Recovery and Azure Backup.
Analyze the differences between Azure App Service, Azure Functions, and Azure Kubernetes Service (AKS) for application hosting.
Learn how to implement governance using Azure Policy, Blueprints, and Management Groups.
Review monitoring tools including Azure Monitor, Log Analytics, and Application Insights.
Build a lab environment to implement the architectures you are studying to gain hands-on experience.
exam_day_tips: Carefully read the case study scenarios; they often contain critical constraints that dictate the correct architectural choice.
Manage your time strictly; if a question is too complex, flag it and move forward to ensure you complete all sections.
Pay close attention to keywords like 'minimum administrative effort' or 'lowest cost' as they change the correct answer.
Use the elimination method for multiple-choice questions to narrow down the most viable architectural solution.
Ensure your testing environment is quiet and your system requirements meet the Pearson VUE standards if taking the exam remotely.
Read the 'Yes/No' series questions carefully, as you cannot go back to previous questions in these specific blocks.
recommended_experience: At least 3-5 years of professional experience in cloud architecture and a strong foundation in Azure administration.
